text.title: competitive-analysis Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for competitive-analysis as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Only analyzing direct competitors — missing inspiration from adjacent industries
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cross-industry patterns produce the most differentiated ideas. A fintech product studying Airbnb's trust signals or Duolingo's onboarding may find patterns no direct competitor has tried.

failure-mode.description: Copying competitor features without understanding why they made that design decision
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A competitor's feature may exist due to legacy technical constraints, regulatory requirements, or past A/B test results that no longer apply. Copying without context means inheriting their tradeoffs.

failure-mode.description: One-time analysis treated as permanent truth
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Competitor products update continuously. An analysis from 6 months ago may reflect a product that no longer exists. Schedule quarterly lightweight checks and annual deep analyses.

failure-mode.description: Treating industry consensus as validated best practice
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If all competitors share a bad pattern, the analysis surfaces a shared bad practice, not a best practice. App store reviews and user research are needed to determine whether the consensus pattern actually works for users.

failure-mode.description: Agents analyzing competitor public pages via screenshots cannot access gated features requiring accounts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The most important flows (checkout, settings, admin) are invisible to the agent. The deepest user flows remain inaccessible without human research participation.

failure-mode.description: App Store reviews are biased toward extreme experiences (1-star and 5-star).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent-extracted patterns may overrepresent edge cases. Combine review extraction with direct product usage for balanced insight.

failure-mode.description: Competitor feature presence detected from marketing pages may not reflect actual implementation quality.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "AI-powered search" on a landing page means nothing about UX quality. Feature presence alone does not validate whether it works well for users.

failure-mode.description: Feature comparison matrices generated by agents from web content frequently contain outdated information.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every cell needs a human spot-check date. Competitor products update faster than manual analysis can track.

failure-mode.description: An agent identifying "what most competitors do" as best practice may be identifying a shared bad practice.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Industry consensus is not the same as user-validated design. Combine competitive findings with app store reviews and user research.
